learning_outcomes.description: Achieve manual dexterity skills appropriate for multi-surface intra-coronal restorations.
Conduct and record a thorough patient interview and clinical examination of the oral cavity and associated structures.
Explain the importance of person-centred practices for the provision of effective healthcare.
Create evidence-based and tailored preventive management plans for common dental diseases.
Apply understanding of head and neck anatomy, oral biology and oral histology to the effective management of pain and delivery of local anaesthetic agents.
Develop self-reflexivity in a simulated clinical context with patients from diverse social and cultural backgrounds.

assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical/peer clinic performance at the interim stage and provide formative feedback to support improvement before the final clinical competence assessment at the end of semester.

Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s clinical performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for the interim clinical practice performance task to be assessed. Detailed assessment criteria and rubrics will be provided on Blackboard.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the Professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domains 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Please note:

  • Written feedback will be provided for any areas to improve as this is formative. Students must reflect on feedback and demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant peer-clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator pathways and documented and taken into consideration for the assessment outcomes.


There is no resubmission or resit available for this component. This is a formative assessment to enable the student to receive feedback on their developing clinical competency.

Any significant clinical concerns pertaining to patient safety will be escalated via clinical communication pathways to the course coordinator and recorded on this report to be considered for the assessment outcome.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Detailed marking rubric for this assessment is available on Blackboard. See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for the portfolio to be assessed for this clinical practice performance task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Pre-clinical competency assessment I: Approximal Posterior Resin Preparation and Restoration
assessments.task_description:
Students must satisfactorily complete a two -surface intra-coronal preparation and restoration on specified posterior tooth/teeth in preclinic sim lab on a mannikin at the end of the specific module in Semester 2. 

Student's hand skill development and feedback provided in each session leading up to this summative task should be used for self-improvement and reflection for optimum performance in summative practical assessment. 

The rubric used for the marking of this assessment will be available in Blackboard. 

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

This is a must pass assessment item, and the student will be provided one resit, with a maximum final mark equal to 50% of total mark available for this assessment after the resit. Students should make themselves available for the resit when announced.The resit date cannot be deferred or re-scheduled

assessments.assessment_title: Preclinical competency assessment II - Class III/IV
assessments.task_description:
Students must satisfactorily complete a simple intra-coronal preparation and a restoration on specified anterior tooth/teeth in preclinic sim lab on a mannikin at the end of module 2. 

Student's hand skill development and feedback provided in each session leading up to this summative task should be used for self-improvement and reflection for optimum performance in summative practical assessment. 

This is a must pass assessment item, and the student will be provided one resit, with a maximum final mark equal to 50% of total mark available for this assessment after the resit. Students should make themselves available for the resit when announced. The resit date cannot be deferred or re-scheduled.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site.

assessments.task_description:
The 15-minute examination with two examiners will assess each student's knowledge, understanding and ability in general dentistry at a level appropriate for end of year 2 BDSc(Hons).

This assessment task will assess at the appropriate level, each student's knowledge, understanding, and ability in general dentistry including basic patient assessment and examination, range of diagnostic tests, rationale for early clinical decision making, basic care planning and sequencing treatment, rationale for treatment of dental caries, non-carious tooth surface loss, early pre and peer clinical skills including choice of materials, patient management, ethics and professionalism.

All concepts learnt in preclinic, peer clinical sessions, workshops and seminars in Semester 2 mapped to course learning outcomes, will be examined. 

Students can expect to answer questions, draw and explain diagrams / models, perform short practical tasks and demonstrate concepts by role-playing. All aspects explored in Semester 2 may be examined. 

This not a MUST PASS assessment.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical and peer clinic performance for this course, at the end of the semester. Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s pre-clinical /peer clinic performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

Students are advised to review any feedback provided in the interim report, to improve pre-clinical/peer clinic performance, in the latter part of his course, to pass this final clinical practice performance assessment which is must pass to pass this course.

Detailed marking rubric for this assessment is available on Blackboard.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for this clinical practice performance task to be assessed. The submitted portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain as per the criteria and standards.

The clinical portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

Passing requirements:

To pass this assessment, students must achieve a pass on this summative assessment on clinical performance progress at this stage. To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domain 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Students must complete all required components as outlined in Submission Guidelines for the portfolio submission.

Please note:

  • Written feedback provided for any areas to improve from the interim task, must be reflected on and student should demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester to enable passing of this must pass task.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator communication pathways and documented in the report and taken into consideration for the final assessment outcome.


To pass this assessment item, students must actively participate in a minimum of 90% of pre-clinic sessions and 90% of peer-clinic sessions as per published rosters and timetables. All allocated tasks in each session must be completed successfully and allocated clinical educator must deem them to be satisfactory.

In accordance with ﾠPPL Assessment Procedure Section 3 Part J,ﾠ supplementary assessment will not be available for this final clinical practice performance task, as professional competencies are developed over a 13-week practicum. There is no resubmission or resit option available for this assessment either.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The submitted portfolio must be COMPLETE in all the following respects and submitted by the published due date which will then be assessed as part of this assessment task. Any incomplete or missing information can result in a fail grade in this must pass assessment task.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 10(27 Jul - 11 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Sessions

A workshop session will be held one afternoon each week to discuss clinical cases in the form of virtual patients. Discussions will also be based on self-directed learning content to familiarise students with the correlation between science and clinical activities.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05

Discussions will also be based on self-directed learning content to familiarise students with the correlation between science and clinical activities.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Preclinical Laboratory

 Series of preclinical skills simulation activities designed to build manipulative and materials handling skills required to begin clinical practice of minimal intervention dentistry. Students will apply knowledge of head and neck anatomy, neurophysiology and pharmacology of local anaesthetic agents to deliver effective pain relief for restorative treatments in simulated environment.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Preclinical Laboratory Series of preclinical skills simulation activities designed to build manipulative and materials handling skills required to begin clinical practice of minimal intervention dentistry. Students will apply knowledge of head and neck anatomy, neurophysiology and pharmacology of local anaesthetic agents to deliver effective pain relief for restorative treatments in simulated environment.

Peer clinic participation (Peer Clinic)

A series of peer clinic sessions designed to build clinical and patient management skills required to begin clinical practice of minimal intervention dentistry.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05, L06
